Ad Text:
About the role

Working as Team Lead – Business Performance Analytics (BI) your role will be to work with team members and stakeholder to improve regulatory processes and outcomes showcasing your skills in technical leadership, team management, solution design and architecture with a focus on producing quality outcomes.

Key responsibilities of this role are:

Act as a hands-on technical lead for the team that models and represents insights and data products in support of data-driven decision making at the NHVR.
In collaboration with the Data Engineering and Data Platform teams, be accountable for the production and maintenance of analytical data models in the Enterprise Data Platform.
Provide authoritative advice to Managers, Directors, and other senior staff across the organisation in creating and using products that support data-driven decision making.
Manage the development of project plans from rough business requirements and collaborate across multi-disciplinary teams to adhere to project roadmaps.
Write technical specifications using industry standard formats, peer review technical writing of team members, and ensure architectural diagrams and technical specifications are up to date.
Participate, collaborate, and help facilitate Agile Program Management processes that foster high performing SCRUM teams to deliver key BAU activities and milestones from the Project Roadmap and Strategic plans.

NHVR’s Data Science and Analytics Unit are currently undertaking growth and considerable transformation, and this is an exciting time to join the organisation.

To learn more, the position description can be found on the NHVR Careers site - https://careers.nhvr.gov.au/jobs/search


About You

We are seeking a dedicated professional with relevant tertiary degree and a minimum of 7 years IT experience and at least 2 years in a technical supervisory capacity.

You will have extensive experience in Business Intelligence, reporting, analysis and data modelling coupled with experience with cloud-based data services and data warehouses (e.g., Microsoft Azure services, Databricks – ideally holding certifications in both).

Your toolkit for success:

Demonstrated experience in forming and leading high-performance technical teams.
Demonstrated extensive skills and experience in structuring and presenting complex data findings and technical decisions to non-technical and executive stakeholders in a clear, persuasive narrative.
Demonstrated extensive skills and experience in analytical data modelling and manipulation.
Demonstrated extensive skills and experience with Microsoft Power BI and related technologies (SQL Server, Integration Services, Azure Data Factory, Databricks, and DevOps).
Proficiency in a programming language like Python, R, PYSQL, PYSpark for data processing, scripting and automation.
Proven aptitude at evaluating and applying new technologies or methods to address future business needs.

 
Why join us?

The National Heavy Vehicle Regulator (NHVR) commenced operations in 2013 as the national entity responsible for regulating all vehicles in Australia over 4.5 tonnes gross vehicle mass. The NHVR minimises the compliance burden on the heavy vehicle transport industry, reduces duplication and inconsistences across state and territory borders, and ultimately for the heavy vehicle business with government in Australia.

We are headquartered in Brisbane and employ more than 1000 people across the ACT, New South Wales, Queensland, South Australia, Tasmania and Victoria.


We offer-

A competitive salary package from $148,069.84 + superannuation
Monthly RDO and paid leave over Christmas closure.
Hybrid working environment – up to 2 days from home.
Up to15.25% superannuation through a co-contribution scheme
Leave loading 17.5%
Wellbeing programs – discounted fitness through Fitness Passport, Discount health insurance, flu vaccine, financial programs including discounted banking products, finance information sessions.
Salary packaging options including novated lease.
Discounted IT products including Microsoft and free family password security plan.
Ongoing training and professional development support
Professional membership

 

Application Process

Please apply with your CV and cover letter addressing the essential requirements of the role.

Applications close: 5.00pm 1st April 2026 (applications may close earlier than the listed closing date if suitable candidate/s are identified prior).

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to contact the NHVR recruitment team on recruitment@nhvr.gov.au.

Successful applicants will be required to complete standard pre-employment checks including a national police and work rights check.

Please Note: We will not require any support from Recruitment Agencies at this time and kindly request no contact is made. Only direct applications will be considered.

We are committed to being an inclusive, diverse and flexible workplace where differences are valued. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experience and abilities.

The Opportunity

Mission Data (MD) Division within the Australian Signals Directorate (ASD) is responsible for providing the foundational data capabilities to support all of ASD’s functions. MD Division does this via the provision of data platforms and services, management of ASD’s mission data, and providing complex analytic development and support, including Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities. All roles within MD require extensive knowledge of and compliance with legislative frameworks, government decision-making and Defence’s mission and policies requirements.

Teamwork, communication, and problem solving skills are essential as you apply your technical skills to a range of data engineering tasks and a constantly evolving technology ecosystem. You will work in a multi-disciplinary environment on some of ASD’s most important data capabilities that support intelligence, cyber security, and offensive cyber operations.

ASD 4 Data Engineer

Assist in the design and implementation of data flows to connect operational systems and analytics platforms, ensuring compliance with data security and governance requirements.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of components of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering solutions.
Work with stakeholders to gather and prepare medium scale data sets that meet defined business requirements.
Support the build and operation of data streaming pipelines.
Undertake initial root cause analysis on data issues and processes under guidance.
Contribute to the development and application of data engineering frameworks, standards and best practices.
Support data model development and assist in making improvements aligned with business needs.
Plan own work, maintain task schedules and provide routine progress updates to supervisors.

ASD 5 Data Engineer

Lead the design and implementation of complex data flows that integrate operational systems with analytics and business intelligence environments, ensuring robust data security and governance.
Oversee the design, development and maintenance of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering solutions, ensuring alignment with architectural direction.
Engage with diverse stakeholders to understand analytical requirements and assemble large, complex data sets that support strategic and operational decision making.
Lead the build, optimisation and maintenance of data streaming systems.
Conduct detailed root cause analysis to resolve complex data issues and identify enhancements to processes and systems.
Develop and refine data engineering frameworks, standards and best practices to improve data availability, performance and security.
Lead the creation and optimisation of data models in response to evolving business requirements.
Develop and manage work plans, coordinate activities across contributors, and report on progress, risks and outcomes.

ASD 6 Data Engineer

Provide skilled leadership in the design and implementation of complex, enterprise wide data flows and integrations, ensuring they meet strategic, security and governance requirements.
Lead and direct the architectural design, development and long term management of enterprise data warehouse and data engineering platforms.
Shape strategic engagement with senior stakeholders to identify emerging data needs and design solutions that support organisational priorities.
Oversee the design and delivery of scalable, enterprise grade data streaming capabilities and advanced data processing pipelines.
Lead complex, Agency wide root cause analysis activities to resolve systemic data issues and drive continuous improvement.
Set the strategic direction for data engineering frameworks, standards and governance processes, ensuring consistent adoption across teams.
Provide authoritative guidance on data model design and evolution for enterprise systems.
Lead program level planning, manage priorities across teams or projects, and provide high level reporting on outcomes, risks and alignment to strategic objectives.

Further information can be found at: I’m changing my career | Australian Signals Directorate (asd.gov.au).

ASD is seeking applicants to fill current and anticipated vacancies and to create a merit pool for future vacancies. In line with the Australian Public Service Commissioner’s Direction 2022, upon completion of the recruitment activity, the merit pool will be available to locations across Australia.

Our Ideal Candidate

Our ideal candidate can have industry experience, formal training or a particular domain expertise. At the core of your skillset, we are looking for an understanding of the fundamentals of data and data engineering.

Ideally, you will have demonstrated use of:

Contemporary best practice with regards to Big Data principles.
Data engineering practices and data technologies.
Strong critical thinking, excellent prioritisation, organisation and teamwork skills.

Application Closing Date: Monday 6 April 2026

For further information please review the job information pack, reference ASD/01618/26 on www.asd.gov.au/careers.

About the Company

Future Property Co Pty Ltd, trading as Henderson Advocacy, is a rapidly growing Australian property advisory and buyer advocacy firm. The company provides property investment advisory, market analysis, and acquisition services across multiple Australian markets.

As part of its digital transformation strategy, the company is investing in artificial intelligence and automation technologies to enhance data analysis, client engagement, and internal operational efficiency.

About the Role

We are seeking an AI Implementation & Integration Specialist (Software Engineer) to design and develop AI-powered applications and automation tools that support business operations.

The successful candidate will be responsible for building scalable AI solutions, integrating machine learning systems into existing platforms, and supporting the development of internal technology tools that improve data analysis, client management, and workflow automation.

Key Responsibilities

· Design and develop AI-enabled software applications and internal automation systems.

· Build and deploy machine learning models and AI applications for business data analysis.

· Develop APIs and backend services to integrate AI tools with existing business platforms.

· Implement conversational AI systems, including chatbots or voice-enabled agents.

· Develop data processing pipelines and integrate structured and unstructured data sources.

· Work with business stakeholders to translate operational requirements into technical solutions.

· Monitor system performance and continuously improve deployed AI applications.

· Collaborate with cross-functional teams to implement scalable software solutions

Required Skills and Experience

· Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, Software Engineering or related field.

· Experience in software development using programming languages such as Python, JavaScript or similar.

· Experience developing APIs or backend systems using frameworks such as FastAPI, Flask, Node.js or similar.

· Familiarity with machine learning or AI frameworks and tools.

· Experience integrating AI solutions into real-world applications or business systems.

· Understanding of database technologies and data processing workflows.

· Strong analytical, troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.

· Ability to work collaboratively within a team environment

Desirable Skills

· At least 1 year experience with AI automation tools or conversational AI systems.

· Experience working with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP.

· Knowledge of data pipelines, vector databases or AI model deployment

How to Apply

Please submit your resume and cover letter through the application link.

Melbourne is Australia's fastest growing city, and public transport is vital to keeping it moving. Since 2009, Metro has proudly operated and maintained a world-class train service and delivered major transport projects that shape the future of our city. Guided by our value of Safely Always, we are one team dedicated to ensuring everyone gets home safely while helping to connect and create a better Melbourne.





We are seeking a Systems Engineer to join the Engineering and Assurance Division, working within the Asset Management team on major railway integration and infrastructure programs across the network.

This role will contribute to the integration and assurance of complex rail systems associated with major infrastructure projects and the ongoing performance of operational assets.

About the Role

As a Systems Engineer, you will support the integration of new and upgraded railway infrastructure into the operational network. Working across multiple asset classes and stakeholders, you will ensure that engineering requirements, asset information, configuration management and lifecycle considerations are appropriately defined and verified.

The role will involve engagement with major capital programs, including large multi-package projects that reshape network infrastructure and support future expansion of the Melbourne rail system.

You will work closely with engineering teams, maintenance divisions and project stakeholders to ensure that assets are introduced, managed and maintained in accordance with Metro's engineering assurance and asset management frameworks.

Please note this is a 12 month fixed term contract.

Key Responsibilities

Apply systems engineering principles across multiple railway asset classes
Support the integration and verification of new infrastructure and asset builds
Contribute to asset requirements definition and verification
Maintain and support configuration management of railway assets
Participate in maintenance strategy development and lifecycle planning
Support asset performance monitoring and continuous improvement initiatives
Contribute to systems assurance and engineering governance processes
Support the development of long-term asset strategies and the 30-year asset plan
Work collaboratively with Rolling Stock, Infrastructure and Operational Control & Management Systems teams

About You

You are an engineer who enjoys working at the interface between infrastructure projects, operational systems and asset management.

You bring strong analytical capability and an interest in how complex infrastructure systems are integrated, managed and optimised across their lifecycle.

Bachelor of Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical or Electronic)
Experience in asset lifecycle or infrastructure engineering
Experience working in major infrastructure or engineering projects
Understanding of asset lifecycle management and performance optimisation
Knowledge of technical standards, risk management and safety environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration skills
Experience in rail or transport infrastructure
Familiarity with project planning tools (MS Project)
Experience using data and performance analysis tools such as Power BI
Exposure to asset information management or digital engineering environments

Next Steps

If this role resonates with your values, experience, and career growth, then please apply before the due date and be part of Melbourne's growing rail network!

Please note applications for this role will close on Thursday 2nd April 2026 but shortlisting will commence prior to the advertisement closing.

Metro is committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process for candidates with specific access needs. Candidates who require adjustments are encouraged to declare this in their application, so that the contact person for the position can discuss your needs.

About QIC

As one of Australias leading institutional investment managers with more than $100 billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.

At QIC, we believe in shared value - where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together.

Life at QIC

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:
Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace
Recharge leave - additional 3 days leave per year
Annual Fitness/home office reimbursement
Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance
26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave
Financial study assistance and study leave
Community leave for supporting the causes you love
Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth.
At QIC, we welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, the LGBTQI+ community, people with disabilities and people from all cultural backgrounds. We have an equitable, barrier-free application process so please inform us of your accessibility requirements in your application or you can reach out to our Talent Team on 07 3360 3800.

About the role 

 

Within QIC’s Operations and Technology team, we are focused on enabling the organisation through smart, reliable, and forward-looking technology solutions. We leverage technology, data, and emerging capabilities such as artificial intelligence to improve how we operate, drive efficiency, and deliver better outcomes for our business and stakeholders.  

 

We are seeking two experienced AI Solutions Developers to lead the design, development, and delivery of AI technologies including Machine Learning (ML), Agentic AI, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs). This role is focused on building production-ready solutions, not just prototypes. You will be responsible for taking AI solutions through the entire development lifecycle, from architecture and design through testing, deployment, and operationalisation. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys solving complex technical problems, working closely with stakeholders, and delivering robust AI solutions that create real business value. 

 

Key responsibilities 

 

Design, build, and implement AI solutions that operate reliably in enterprise environments. 
Translate business requirements into scalable AI architectures and working solutions. 
Lead the end-to-end development lifecycle, including design, build, testing, deployment, and ongoing optimisation. 
Work closely with business stakeholders, business analysts and technical teams to ensure solutions meet operational needs. 
Develop production-ready systems using Microsoft technologies and cloud platforms. 
Ensure strong documentation, auditability, and compliance across all development work. 
Manage and support change release processes within enterprise software environments. 
Identify and resolve complex technical challenges throughout development and deployment. 
Implement testing strategies to ensure reliability, performance, and security. 
Maintain strong attention to detail in architecture, implementation, and documentation. 

 

About you 

 

Proven experience across the full application development lifecycle: design, development, testing, implementation, and handover. 
Strong background in AI/ML development, neural networks and deep learning techniques.  
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C#, R or Java.  
Strong knowledge of cloud platforms for AI deployment (specifically Azure, Power Platform, CoPilot Studio, and Foundry).  
Experience working within enterprise environments, including software delivery processes, change management and release cycles, governance and audit requirements. 
Demonstrated ability to solve complex technical problems across multiple systems, with strong technical documentation skills. 
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills.

Company Overview

AEM is an engineering manufacturer in the electrical infrastructure industry. We are leaders in the field of transformer core design and core manufacturing machinery for the Australian and international markets. AEM is an innovative SME and we are seeking a dynamic and experienced Research, Design and Development Engineer to join our engineering team.

Position Overview

The role requires experience in a manufacturing environment, and you will be responsible for Conceptual and detail design of mechanical/mechatronic components, assemblies and machines. Taking design concepts through the design and conceptual stage to prototyping and testing.

Effective communication and coordination with other departments, such as production and finance, are crucial to ensure that the process runs smoothly and cost-effectively.

As a Research, Design and Development Engineer at AEM Cores, you will drive advancements in transformer core design and automated core manufacturing machinery, helping improve the efficiency, performance, and reliability of transformers used across modern power networks.

Working closely with engineering and production teams, you will transform ideas into practical solutions that enhance manufacturing capability and support the evolving demands of the global energy sector.




We can offer you:

·         Full time position

·         Varied duties with on-the-job training and opportunity to advance skills

·         Reputable organisation with a highly skilled team

·         Competitive rates

·         Supportive, flexible and collaborative work environment

Principle responsibilities:

·         Design new equipment, coordinate reviews of that equipment and organise manufacture and commissioning.

·         Establish test methods, evaluate engineering test results and reports to determine if design meets functional and performance criteria.

·         Creation of 3D CAD models and engineering drawings using Inventor and AutoCAD software.

·         External supplier liaison, research, sourcing and purchasing of new engineering materials and products.

·         Management of engineering projects including problem solving and resource allocation.

·         Engineering document creation and publishing onto the company Technical Database.

·         Co-ordinate project flow and report on costs and schedules of engineering projects.

·         Assist in the creation of product manuals and marketing materials.

·         Contribute to the development, implementation and achievement of AEM’s overall business objectives, strategies and plans;

·         Other duties as required.

·          

Essential criteria:

·         Bachelor degree in Mechatronic Engineering or equivalent

·         Proven 3-5 years relevant R&D/product development experience in a manufacturing environment

·         Proven experience in  the automation field

·         Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 Desirable criteria:

·         Familiarity with AutoDesk Inventor, PLC Software and Australian and International machine design safety standards is highly desirable.

·         There may be a requirement for overseas travel for project commissioning

For more information about AEM Cores visit www.aemcores.com.au

AEM is committed to a safe work environment and our hiring process includes reference checks, police checks and pre-employment medicals and drug and alcohol screening.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist – Applied AI & Data Engineering
Ad Text:
This is: the job
We’re looking for a hands-on builder, someone who thinks like a data engineer or software developer first, and a data scientist second.

This is a 12-month initial contract based in Melbourne, with strong potential to extend, working within a large enterprise environment delivering real AI capability at scale.

This role sits at the intersection of AI, data engineering and software development, focused on building production-grade AI systems from the ground up. You won’t be handing off notebooks, you’ll be owning problems end-to-end, from messy raw data through to deployed, scalable solutions.

The work centres around LLMs, NLP, recommendation systems and modern AI pipelines, all within an enterprise Azure environment. If you enjoy building robust systems, writing clean code, and shipping solutions that actually get used, this is for you.

This is: what you’ll do
Design and build end-to-end AI and data pipelines, from ingestion through to production deployment
Develop embedding-based search and recommendation systems, including vector databases and similarity search
Build and deploy NLP and LLM pipelines to extract insights from unstructured data
Write production-quality, scalable code, not one-off scripts or notebooks
Own and maintain data and ML infrastructure, including orchestration, monitoring and reliability
Implement MLOps practices such as CI/CD, model monitoring, drift detection and retraining
Work closely with stakeholders to translate technical solutions into real business impact
Contribute to engineering standards, code quality and system design across the team
This is: what you’ll need
5+ years’ experience in a hands-on role spanning data engineering, ML engineering or software development
Strong programming skills in Python, with a focus on writing clean, production-ready code
Strong proficiency in the modern AI and ML stack including PyTorch or TensorFlow, HuggingFace Transformers, scikit-learn, pandas and related tools

Job Title: AI Automation Engineer | Full Stack Developer
Ad Text:
Welcome to Lakestone.ai, Perth's fastest growing AI agency.

We build complete AI-driven production systems for corporate clients, and we're very good at it.

We're a small, dedicated, passionate, high-output team but first and foremost we're about developing a fun, engaging culture of learning and sharing.

 

What we offer:

Competitive salary + performance-based incentives/bonuses

Genuine flexibility - work where and when you're most productive

Variety - Interesting, varied work across multiple industries, projects, and tech stacks

A brilliant team - you'll learn from — and who'll learn from you!

 

The work:

No two weeks look the same. Our clients trust us with their most complex problems and data. You'll work across multiple projects, multiple stacks and multiple industries.

Recent builds include:

Custom CRM dashboards with real-time analytics

RAG pipelines with pgvector and semantic search

Speech-to-text processing systems

AI-powered chatbots and conversational agents

Internal tooling that replaces entire manual workflows

 

How we operate:

Output is the only metric. No standups. No timesheets. No micromanagement.

You get flexibility, autonomy and performance bonuses. We care about what you ship, not when you're online.

Some days you'll be at a client's office, sitting with their team, understanding the problem firsthand and shipping solutions in real time.

Other days you'll be working from home, heads down on a build. You manage your own schedule — the balance between client-facing and deep work is yours to own.

Note: This means two things: you need to be genuinely comfortable engaging with non-technical clients and end-users face-to-face, and you need to be excellent at managing yourself.

 

Location:

Perth-based only. You will need to be at client sites regularly. Candidates must have full working rights in Australia to be considered.

 

The Requirements (there are only two):

(1) You must know Claude Code inside and out.

You need to demonstrate deep, working knowledge of:

Customisation - Skills, hooks, MCP servers, rules, and custom agents

Multi-worktree workflows — planning, execution, and review running simultaneously

Memory systems — project memory, session context management, episodic/semantic/hybrid

Model tiering — orchestrating Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku across parallel sub-agents

Third-party integrations — MCPs, skills, and agent ecosystems




(2) Show us what you've built.

No lengthy CVs. No cover letters. No "proficient in AI tools." No X+ years of previous software experience or university degrees required.

We want to see a repo, a demo, or a screen recording. Show us something that proves you build real things with AI-assisted development. We'll be looking at your architecture, security practices, code structure, and how you manage complexity — this will be our reference check.




The Tech Stack:

You should be very comfortable across most of these:

- TypeScript, React, Vite, Tailwind CSS

- Hono, Express, Node.js, Bun

- Supabase (Postgres), MongoDB

- Vercel, Cloudflare (Workers, Pages), Railway, Fly

- Docker (local + production)

- GitHub Actions, CI/CD pipelines

- Microsoft 365 tenant administration

 

How to Apply:

Skip the SEEK Apply button. Send an email directly to join@lakestone.ai with your contact details and links to your best work—repos, demos, screen recordings, or architecture diagrams. This is what we'll evaluate.
